_meta.purpose: Wind clause corpus for DBR checks D13 (wind parameters) and D21 (dynamic/wind-tunnel). Encoded from clean screenshots supplied 2026-06-08.
_meta.covers: Cl 6.3 design wind speed chain (k1-k4 + Tables 1-3), Cl 6.4-6.6, Cl 7.1-7.3 design wind pressure (pz, pd, Kd, Ka, Kc + Table 4), internal/external pressure-coefficient framing. Added: Cl 10 dynamic wind response (along/across, gust factor, peak accel), Table 36 damping, Cl 8 interference, force-coefficient tables for towers/frames/members.
_meta.still_missing: OPTIONAL only: Annex A (basic wind speed Vb city/town list) - redundant given BMTPC Atlas is the location source. Pressure-coefficient Tables 5/6/7 now encoded (verify flags set). Core D13/D21 wind chain complete.

clauses.title: Design Wind Speed (Vz)
clauses.statement: The basic wind speed Vb (from Fig. 1 / Annex A) is modified for risk level, terrain-roughness and height, local topography, and cyclonic importance to obtain the design wind speed Vz at height z.
clauses.formula: Vz = Vb * k1 * k2 * k3 * k4
clauses.where: Vz = design wind speed at height z (m/s); k1 = risk/probability coefficient (6.3.1); k2 = terrain-roughness & height factor (6.3.2); k3 = topography factor (6.3.3); k4 = importance factor for cyclonic region (6.3.4).
clauses.note: Wind speed may be taken as constant up to 10 m height; for buildings <10 m, pressures may be reduced 20% for stability/framing design.

clauses.title: Risk Coefficient (k1 Factor)
clauses.statement: Fig. 1 gives basic wind speed for terrain Category 2 at 10 m, based on 50-year mean return period. k1 adjusts for design life / class of structure. For ordinary buildings a 50-year return period (k1 = 1.0) is used.

clauses.title: Risk Coefficients (k1) for Different Classes of Structures in Different Wind Speed Zones

ii_temporary.class: Temporary sheds, formwork/falsework, structures during construction, boundary walls

iii_low_hazard.class: Low-hazard (isolated towers in wooded areas, farm buildings other than residential)

clauses.title: Terrain, Height Factor (k2)
clauses.statement: Terrain category (1-4) is selected by ground-surface roughness. Category 1 = exposed open terrain (z0=0.002 m); Category 2 = open terrain, scattered obstructions 1.5-10 m (z0=0.02 m); Category 3 = numerous closely spaced obstructions, building-size, up to 10 m (z0=0.2 m); Category 4 = numerous large high closely spaced obstructions, large city centres, obstructions >25 m (z0=2.0 m). k2 multiplies Vb to give wind speed at height z per Table 2.

clauses.title: Factors to Obtain Design Wind Speed Variation with Height in Different Terrains (k2)
Topography (k3 Factor)
clauses.statement: k3 accounts for local topographic features (hills, valleys, cliffs, escarpments, ridges) not captured by Vb. k3 = 1.0 when the upwind slope theta <= 3 degrees; for slopes > 3 degrees, k3 is in the range 1.0 to 1.36 (method in Annex C). k3 is maximum near the ground and reduces to 1.0 at higher levels.

clauses.title: Importance Factor for Cyclonic Region (k4)
clauses.statement: For the east coast of India and Gujarat coast (cyclone-prone, ~60 km belt), k4 is applied per importance of structure.

clauses.title: Design Wind Pressure
clauses.statement: Wind pressure at height z is derived from design wind speed; the design wind pressure pd applies directionality, area-averaging and combination factors, with a floor of 0.70*pz.
clauses.formula: pz = 0.6 * Vz^2  (N/m^2, Vz in m/s);   pd = Kd * Ka * Kc * pz;   pd shall not be less than 0.70 * pz
clauses.where: Kd = wind directionality factor (7.2.1); Ka = area averaging factor (7.2.2); Kc = combination factor (see 7.3.3.13).
clauses.note: Coefficient 0.6 (SI) reflects average Indian atmospheric conditions.

clauses.title: Wind Directionality Factor (Kd)
clauses.statement: Accounts for randomness in wind direction relative to direction-specific pressure/force coefficients.

clauses.title: Area Averaging Factor (Ka)
clauses.statement: Pressure coefficients in 7.3 are area-averaged; larger tributary areas give lower correlated pressure, reduced via Ka (Table 4).

clauses.title: Area Averaging Factor (Ka)
clauses.note: Linear interpolation for intermediate tributary areas.

clauses.title: Wind Load on Individual Members
clauses.statement: Wind load normal to an individual structural element or cladding unit accounts for the pressure difference between opposite faces (external minus internal).
clauses.where: Cpe = external pressure coefficient; Cpi = internal pressure coefficient; A = surface area of element/cladding unit; pd = design wind pressure.
clauses.sign_convention: Positive wind load = force toward the element; negative = away.

clauses.title: Internal Pressure Coefficients (Cpi)
clauses.statement: Cpi depends on the permeability/openings of the cladding. The internal coefficient is added algebraically to the external; the case producing greater distress governs.

clauses.title: External Pressure Coefficients (Cpe)
clauses.statement: 7.3.3.1 walls of rectangular clad buildings per Table 5 (+ local concentration coefficients); 7.3.3.2 pitched/hipped/mono-slope roofs per Table 6 (Table 7 for mono-slope); 7.3.3.3 canopy roofs per Tables 8 and 9.
clauses.note: Pressure-coefficient route. For overall wind force on a clad tower the force-coefficient method (Fig.4 / Table 25) is normally used instead.

clauses.title: Dynamic Wind Response - General
clauses.statement: Tall buildings that are wind-sensitive shall be designed for dynamic wind loads using hourly mean wind speed as reference. Along-wind load effects (bending moments, shear forces, tip deflections) use the Gust Factor (GF) method per 10.2. Across-wind design peak base overturning moment and tip deflection are calculated per 10.3.

clauses.title: Along Wind Response (Gust Factor Method)
clauses.statement: Design hourly mean wind pressure at height z is multiplied by the Gust Factor G (dependent on overall height h and level s; for base bending moment and top deflection take s = 0). Design peak along-wind base bending moment Ma is summed from peak along-wind loads Fz over height.
clauses.formula: Ma = SUM (Fz * Z);  Fz = Cf,z * Az * pd_mean * G;  G = 1 + r*sqrt[ gv^2*Bs*(1+g)^2 + (Hs*gR^2*S*E)/beta ]
clauses.where: Cf,z = drag force coefficient of building for area Az; pd_mean = design hourly mean wind pressure = 0.6*Vz,d^2 (N/m2); r = roughness factor = 2x longitudinal turbulence intensity Ih,i (6.5); gv = peak factor for upwind velocity fluctuation = 3.0 (terrain cat 1 & 2), 4.0 (cat 3 & 4); Bs = background factor; gR = peak factor for resonant response = sqrt[2 ln(3600 fa)]; Hs = height factor for resonance = 1+(s/h)^2; S = size reduction factor; E = spectrum of turbulence; beta = damping coefficient (Table 36); fa = first-mode along-wind natural frequency (Hz).

clauses.title: Peak Acceleration in Along Wind Direction
clauses.statement: Peak along-wind acceleration at the top (x_hat, m/s2) for serviceability. Use a mean wind speed Vh for a 5-year mean return period; a reduced damping beta = 0.011 is suggested for RCC structures.
clauses.where: x_mean = mean deflection at the position where acceleration is required.

clauses.title: Across Wind Response
clauses.statement: Gives equivalent static across-wind load and base overturning moment for tall enclosed buildings and towers of rectangular cross-section. Not required for lattice towers. The across-wind design peak base bending moment Mc:
clauses.formula: Mc = 0.5 * gh * ph_mean * b * h^2 * (1.06 - 0.06*k) * sqrt(pi*Cfs/beta)
clauses.where: gh = peak factor = sqrt[2 ln(3600 fc)] in cross-wind direction; ph_mean = hourly mean wind pressure at height h (Pa); b = breadth normal to wind (m); h = height (m); k = mode-shape power exponent; Cfs = across-wind force spectrum coefficient (Fig.10 square section, Fig.11 rectangular 2:1 and 1:2); beta = damping (Table 36); fc = first-mode across-wind natural frequency (Hz).
clauses.note: Cfs read from Fig.10 (square) / Fig.11 (rectangular 2:1, 1:2) vs ratio Vh,d/(fc*b); curves given for turbulence intensity 0.12 and 0.20 at 2/3 h. These are charts (not encoded numerically) - read off the figure for the building aspect ratio.

clauses.title: Peak Acceleration in Across Wind Direction
clauses.statement: Peak across-wind acceleration at the top (y_hat, m/s2) for structures of approximately constant mass per unit height. Across-wind load distribution Fz,c per unit height obtained from Mc by linear distribution.
clauses.formula: y_hat = 1.5 * (gh * ph_mean * b / m0) * (0.76 + 0.24*k) * sqrt(pi*Cfs/beta);   Fz,c = (3*Mc/h^2)*(z/h)
clauses.where: m0 = average mass per unit height (kg/m); Fz,c = across-wind load per unit height at height z.

clauses.title: Combination of Along Wind and Across Wind Load Effects
clauses.statement: The along-wind and across-wind loads have to be applied simultaneously on the building/structure during design.

clauses.title: Suggested Values of Structural Damping Coefficients (beta)
clauses.note: For along-wind peak acceleration (10.2.1) a reduced beta = 0.011 is suggested for RCC structures.

clauses.title: Interference Effects
clauses.statement: Wind interference from neighbouring structures is captured by a wind interference factor (IF), a multiplier on design wind pressure/force. 8.2 low-rise flat-roof buildings: increase up to 25 percent for c/c spacing x = 5b, negligible beyond 20b, linear interpolation between. 8.3 tall buildings: IF zones per Fig.8; given IF are median values for preliminary design only - for final design of tall buildings consult specialist literature or carry out a wind-tunnel study.

clauses.title: Force Coefficients for Clad Buildings
clauses.statement: 7.4.2.1 Clad buildings of uniform section: overall force coefficients for RECTANGULAR clad buildings with flat roofs in uniform flow are given in Fig. 4; for OTHER clad buildings of uniform section (without projections, except where shown) use Table 25. 7.4.2.2 Buildings of circular cross-section use Table 25 (Fig. 5 for circular shapes of infinite length). Overall wind force F = Cf * pd * b * h, where b = breadth normal to wind, h = height.

clauses.title: Force Coefficient (Cf) for Rectangular Clad Building in Uniform Flow
clauses.statement: Cf for a sharp-edged RECTANGULAR clad building, read from the chart as a function of plan ratio a/b and height ratio h/b. This is the governing source for the overall along-wind drag coefficient Cf,z of a rectangular RC tower (plug into Gust Factor eqn 10.2).

axes.x: a/b (plan ratio, a = along-wind plan dimension, b = across-wind breadth)

clauses.behaviour: Cf rises to a peak near a/b ~ 0.6-0.7 then declines as a/b increases; higher h/b gives higher peak (h/b=inf peaks ~2.5-2.7; h/b=1 peaks ~1.3). For h/b<1, Cf ~1.0-1.2.
clauses.note: CHART - exact Cf must be read off the curve for the building a/b and h/b; values above are approximate anchors only, do not hard-code. Used as Cf,z in Fz = Cf,z*Az*pd*G.

clauses.title: Force Coefficients (Cf) for Clad Buildings of Uniform Section (Acting in the Direction of Wind)
clauses.note: Cf for non-rectangular plan shapes. Columns = Cf for height/breadth ratio h/b: ['upto_1/2', '1', '2', '5', '10', '20', 'inf']. Vd*b (m2/s) sets the flow regime per row. For sharp rectangular plans use Fig. 4 instead.

rows._note: 3rd value (h/b=2) read as 0.6 but possibly 0.9 - verify

clauses.title: External Pressure Coefficients (Cpe) for Walls of Rectangular Clad Buildings
clauses.verify_note: Transcribed from screenshots p.15. Surfaces A/B/C/D per plan diagram (theta measured from wind to wall A). h/w>=6 block uncertain (B-cell read as -1.85, looks anomalous) - confirm against PDF before using.

clauses.title: External Pressure Coefficients (Cpe) for Pitched Roofs of Rectangular Clad Buildings
clauses.verify_note: Transcribed from screenshot p.16. Main roof-zone grid (EF,GH at wind 0deg; EG,FH at wind 90deg). Local-coefficient edge-zone columns are dense - verify separately if needed. Applies only to PITCHED roofs (not the flat-roof tower).

clauses.title: External Pressure Coefficients (Cpe) for Monoslope Roofs of Rectangular Clad Buildings (h/w < 2)
clauses.verify_note: Transcribed from screenshot p.17. Columns: wind 0,45,90(*=windward w/2),90(**=remainder),135,180 giving H and L. Local coeffs H1,H2,L1,L2,He,Le. Applies only to MONOSLOPE roofs (not the flat-roof tower).

notes.*: Applied to length w/2 from windward end
notes.1: h = height of eaves at lower side; l = greater horizontal dimension; w = lesser horizontal dimension
notes.2: l and w are overall length and width including overhangs
